answer:
**In "Laaon woh tinke kahin se aashiyane ke liye" (Bang-e-Dara Part 1, Chapter 8), the central conceit portrays the soul (likened to a "soul-bird") contemplating building a nest as a symbol of worldly attachment or a fragile, impermanent home in the material realm.**[^1]

The poem opens with the bird questioning whether to "gather straws to build my nesting-place" amid "restless bolts of lightning" eager to destroy it, highlighting the nest's doomed vulnerability.[^2] Heaven has already "marked that bough for its disgrace," the very spot chosen for this "fragile nesting-place," underscoring inevitable ruin.[^3] The speaker rejects settling there, aspiring for a stronger inner "wish" to repel heavenly destruction and advising a "comrade bird" against building or gathering harvest, as "a bolt of fire will surely come to leave no trace."[^4] Ultimately, the "soul-bird must not sing its anthem of the free" in this garden, unfit for such lofty grace, urging transcendence over earthly nesting.[^5]

This extended metaphor critiques attachment to the transient world, favoring spiritual freedom and divine aspiration—themes resonant in Iqbal's philosophy.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 8 ("Laaon woh tinke kahin se aashiyane ke liye"), [translation](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-73e9bf](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/#p-73e9bf).

[^3]: Ibid., [p-4cee3b](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/#p-4cee3b).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-fcf57b](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/#p-fcf57b) and [p-2dbb50](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/#p-2dbb50).

[^5]: Ibid., [p-da328b](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/#p-da328b) and [p-c4ec62](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/08-laaon-woh-tinke-kahin-se-aashiyane-ke-liye/translation/#p-c4ec62).
